---
title: "Reproducible Analytical Pipelines"
subtitle: "My personal hot takes 🔥"
author: "[Dr Rhian Davies](mailto:statsrhian@gmail.com)"
date: 2026-05-21
format: su-theme-revealjs
execute: 
  eval: false
footer: "{{< fa brands bluesky >}} {{< fa brands mastodon >}} {{< fa brands linkedin >}} [@statsRhian](https://bsky.app/profile/statsrhian.bsky.social) &nbsp; &nbsp; {{< fa laptop-code >}} [rhian.rbind.io](https://rhian.rbind.io)"
---

## 🏛️ My RAP Pillars

- 🔍 **Visible**: Can I find it?

- 🔁 **Reusable**: Can I adapt it?

- 🛡️ **Reliable**: Can I trust it?

## 🔍 Can I find it?

- Public and open 🔓
- Discoverable 🌐
- Clear README & examples 📖
- LICENSE included 📃

## 🔁 Can I adapt it?

- Open-source languages 🔓
- Well documented 📚
- Consistent code style 💅
- Modular 🧩
- Configurable ⚙️
- Easy to install 📦

## 🛡️ Can I trust it?

- Tested 🧪
- Automated checks 🤖
- Peer reviewed 👩‍💻
- Reproducible environments 🌱


# {.center}
![](img/hot-takes.jpg){fig-alt="Two animated characters from Toy Story, Woody and Buzz Lightyear, stand together. Buzz gestures widely. Text reads 'Hot Takes Hot Takes Everywhere.'"}


## {.center}

::: r-fit-text
 Commented code
:::

::: r-fit-text
 $\neq$ good code
:::

##

::: {.panel-tabset}

### ❌ Over-commented

```r
library(dplyr) # load dplyr package
d1 <- as.data.frame(crimtab) # turn table into dataframe
d2 <- d1 |> # create new dataset
  mutate(# make new variables
    fg = f1(Var1), # convert Var1 into numeric this represents finger length
    ht = f1(Var2),     # convert Var2 into numeric this represents height
    n = Freq # rename frequency column this is the count
  ) |>
  select(fg, ht, n) # keep only required columns

# function to turn factor into numeric 
# this is needed because the variables are factors
# and we want numeric values instead
f1 <- function(x) {
  y <- as.character(x)  # convert factor to character
  as.numeric(y)  # convert character to numeric
}
```

### ✅ Self-documenting

```r
library(dplyr)

criminal_summary <- crimtab |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  mutate(
    finger_length = extract_numeric_from_factor(Var1),
    height = extract_numeric_from_factor(Var2),
    count = Freq
  ) |>
  select(finger_length, height, count)

extract_numeric_from_factor <- function(factor) {
  as.numeric(as.character(factor))
}
```

:::

## {.center}

::: r-fit-text
**[Reusable](https://rhian.rbind.io/talks/2024-11-22-reusability/)** ♻️
:::

::: r-fit-text
 not reproducible
:::


## {.center}

::: r-fit-text
Open first 🔓
:::

## {.center}

::: r-fit-text
You [don't need friends](https://github.com/statsrhian/2048) 👩‍💻
:::
::: r-fit-text
to use Git
:::

::: aside
but it helps
:::


## {.center}

::: r-fit-text
[Anyone](https://rhian.rbind.io/talks/2026-02-25-rainbowr-pkg/)
:::

::: r-fit-text
 can build an R package 📦
:::

## Myths ❌

It doesn't have to...

- have hundreds of functions
- be useful for anyone else
- do anything fancy
- be on CRAN
- be scary

## {.center}

::: r-fit-text
[Ditch QA](https://rhian.rbind.io/talks/2025-11-13-qa-in-github/)
:::

::: r-fit-text
(spreadsheets). Make QA work for you.
:::

## {.center}

![](img/yeah-nah-qa.png){fig-alt="Drake Nah-Yeah meme. Nah is a colourful spreadsheet, Yeah is a number of QA tasks in GitHub."}

## {.center}

::: r-fit-text
It doesn't have to be
:::

::: r-fit-text
perfect ✨
:::


## How do I get support? 💙

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="60%"}

- RAP drop-in [sign up](https://www.smartsurvey.co.uk/s/XPUHSJ) & [discussions](https://github.com/The-Strategy-Unit/RAP_Drop_In/discussions)
- RAP [Code Review Buddies Network](https://github.com/The-Strategy-Unit/RAP_Drop_In/wiki/Code-Review-Buddies-Network)
- NHS Open Analytics [slack #rap](https://nhsoacommunity.slack.com/signup#/domain-signup)
- Chat with me @statsRhian 😊

:::

::: {.column width="40%"}

![](img/proud.svg){fig-alt="A cartoon person standing proudly next to a screen"}
:::
